attributes.title: SEO Review (3 months) AU & NZ
attributes.description:
• The SEO Impact Report provides a clear view of progress at 3 and 6 months post-delivery. It benchmarks performance against competitors, validates the results of implemented recommendations, and highlights new opportunities for optimisation.

  • Delivered on a time-and-materials basis at $1,600 per region, per report, it gives Decjuba confidence that investment is translating into measurable outcomes.

  • Deliverables

    • Validation of results at 3 and 6 months, benchmarking progress, highlighting gains, and identifying next opportunities.

attributes.title: Tolstoy Account Structure Review & Training Setup
attributes.description:
Description
The team has raised questions around the optimal Tolstoy setup to support regional product linking and playlists.

Currently, one Tolstoy account has already been created, however we need to confirm whether:

  • A single Tolstoy account with multiple playlists is sufficient, or

  • Multiple Tolstoy accounts are required (likely one per region) to ensure product and Shopify links correctly route to region-specific sites.

Initial assumption is that 3 Tolstoy accounts may be required (one per region) so that all product, PDP, and collection links resolve correctly by region.

This task is to confirm the correct approach and define a clear setup and training path for the client team.

Task Objective
Confirm the correct Tolstoy account structure (single vs multi-account), and prepare the team to self-manage playlists and product linking through training resources.

Scope

  • Review Tolstoy’s capabilities and constraints around:

    • Regional Shopify integrations

    • Product and collection linking by region

    • Playlist reuse vs duplication across regions

  • Confirm whether multiple Tolstoy accounts are required or if one account can support all regions

  • Advise on best-practice account structure going forward

Deliverables

  • Clear recommendation:

    • One Tolstoy account with multiple playlists or

    • Multiple Tolstoy accounts (per region), with rationale

  • Confirmation of how the existing Tolstoy account fits into the recommended setup

  • Training materials to support internal enablement - they would like just a notion page like we usually do with some recorded loom videos on how to use. Rather than a live training session so whole team can watch and learn.

attributes.title: Template Task
attributes.description:
Productive Task Template (Team Standard)

Use this template for ALL new tasks. Tasks missing key sections may be returned for clarification before work begins.

Context / Background

Briefly explain why this task exists and how it fits into the wider project or phase.

  • What problem are we solving?

  • What triggered this task (client request, sprint goal, dependency)?

Objective / Goal

Clearly state the intended outcome of this task.

  • What does “done” look like?

  • What value does this deliver?

  • What does the client want to achieve?

Scope of Work (In Scope)

List exactly what is included in this task.

  • Use clear dot points

  • Be specific and actionable

  • Avoid vague language

Out of Scope

(Optional but recommended)

  • Clearly state what is not included

  • Helps prevent scope creep and rework

Deliverables

List the tangible outputs this task will produce.

  • Files, documents, updates, artefacts

  • Be explicit

Dependencies / Inputs Required

What must be available for this task to start or complete successfully?

  • Access (Figma, GA4, Shopify, etc.)

  • Client inputs or approvals

  • Related tasks that must be completed first

Effort / Estimate Notes

(Optional but encouraged)

  • Assumptions

  • Known risks or unknowns

  • What could increase effort

Links & References

All relevant materials in one place.

  • Figma links

  • Docs

  • Live URLs

  • Related Productive tasks

Notes

  • Sub-tasks should be created for execution steps where relevant

  • One task = one clear outcome

  • If scope changes, update the task description before proceeding

attributes.title: Post Purchase Customer Survey
attributes.description:
Hi @[{"type":"person","id":"992798","label":"Kat Hanzalek","avatar_url":"https://files.productive.io/people/avatars/6a6b9c6e-1224-442f-ae77-a2cfb1107782/thumb/kat.jpg","attachment_url":null,"is_done":false}] and Dot team, 

This is my first task so not sure if I am doing this correctly!

NPFG would like to add a post-purchase DTC customer survey to our project plan for implementation following launch.  Suggested in-market timing Monday, US April 20th. See attached for the current questionnaire.

This was spearheaded by NA team; however, I believe this makes sense for global implementation so adding Kristy to this as an FYI. 

Ideally this would be an embedded / inline form on this site (likely via the "Forms theme app embed" via Shopify). It would link to a third-party service platform. Note, we already pay for SurveyMonkey. If there is a way to fully integrate SurveyMonkey into Shopify, that would be amazing as answers could live in Shopify. 

Would love to review this as a team and determine accurate project scope / strategy. Realize we have lots going on right now with launch, so this is not a priority right now. If we could at minimum add this to our project plan for discussion mid-March, that would be ideal. 

Thanks,
Shannon

CC: @[{"type":"person","id":"1082405","label":"Courtney Butcher","avatar_url":null,"attachment_url":null,"is_done":false}]

attributes.title: Information Architecture
attributes.description:
Overview

The Information Architecture (IA) output provides a clear and agreed structure for the new site. It maps how content and categories will be organised, ensuring customers can easily navigate and find what they need.

This work draws from analytics, audits, and stakeholder input to deliver a finalised sitemap and IA that will guide design, development, and content planning.

Further information

attributes.title: Creative Concepts
attributes.description:
Overview

The Creative Concepts bring to life the visual look and feel of the solution, aligning the brand’s identity with the experience we are designing. Using insights gathered during Discovery – and specifically the Creative Workshop – we create a set of design concepts that define how the solution will look and feel in practice.

This step sets the foundation for the entire design phase, ensuring that the visual direction is fully aligned with the brand’s DNA before moving into full template and component design.

Further information

Actions for Delivery Lead

  • Schedule in internal kickoff to onboard Designer

  • Schedule internal review time & internal feedback

attributes.title: Design Rollout
attributes.description:
Overview

The Design Rollout takes the approved Creative Concepts and applies them across the complete site experience. Every page template, component, and interaction pattern is designed to ensure a consistent, user-focused, and brand-aligned experience.

This stage also produces a comprehensive style guide that defines the visual language and provides the development team with the assets and specifications needed to begin building the site.

Further information

Actions for Delivery Lead

  • DL to schedule internal meeting with TL prior to presenting to client, to ensure designs are achievable and validate designs

  • Schedule client call to present design rollout

attributes.title: Global: New website launch EDM
attributes.description:
New site

We are launching a new site (US: 3 March, AU: 16 March, NZ: 31 March) and need to communicate this to users.

Who: All users who are signed up to emails

What: Inform of the new site and the benefits of it

Where: All Markets (US, AU, NZ)

When: Few days or 1 week following official launch

  • US: 3 March

  • AU: 16 March

  • NZ: 31 March

Why: Share news that we have a new site and new place to shop, include benefits such as being able to shop cat and dog together, new features, new bundles etc.

TBC on whether we share here that they will need to reactivate their account and/or subscription - DC to advise

How: x1 eDM per market (x3 in total)

attributes.title: Global: Reactivating subscriptions eDM
attributes.description:
Reactivating subscriptions

The new website launch will require customers to reactivate their subscriptions and reenter payment details. @[{"type":"person","id":"373912","label":"Ellie White","avatar_url":"https://files.productive.io/people/avatars/18052edf-8dcd-45eb-9e65-68e81931cb1c/thumb/Screenshot_2025-05-23_at_8.33.41%E2%80%AFAM.png","attachment_url":null,"is_done":true}] mentioned there was some work and an existing ticket underway on a recommendation from Dot Collective here, so looking for a recommendation on how to do this following best practice. If via an eDM, then thinking the below:

Who: All users who have a subscription with us

What: Prompt to reactivate their subscription and reenter their payment details

Where: All Markets (US, AU, NZ)

When: Dot to advise, does this need to happen before new site launching? Do not want it to impact the fulfilment of orders during the transition period in any way

Why: Users will need to reactivate their account and reenter payment details. Want to avoid any assumptions that price increase is due to new site etc. and results in people not wanting to reactivate. We likely need to offer a discount or incentive to reactivate, eg $10 OFF discount.

How: x2 eDMs per market (x6 in total)

Notes:

Head’s up price increases coming April 1, update now to ensure set-up before change. For US, version required without mentioning price increase for US to exclude Feline Freeze-Dried customers & K9 Toppers & 17/6oz Freeze-Dried (no increase impacting them). Link to customer service.

attributes.title: NPFG US Site Stat Review
attributes.description:
Hi Kate, see below the email from Shannon

Summary:
The US site has recently launched and is currently stabilising. Initial reporting in Shopify shows lower than expected sessions and conversion rate compared to pre-launch benchmarks.

Context:

  • Previous benchmarks:

    • K9: ~1.5K visitors per day

    • Feline: ~2.5K–3K visitors per day

  • Expected combined traffic should be higher than current Shopify reporting

  • Conversion rate is also lower than anticipated

  • Client noted ~70% of sales are from repeat customers

Request:

  • Validate Shopify session tracking and conversion rate accuracy

  • Compare pre vs post launch performance across key metrics

  • Identify any potential gaps in tracking, attribution, or data capture

  • Assess if current performance could be normal post-launch volatility or if further investigation is required

attributes.title: Investigation: Fix & Standardise Sample Request Tracking (GA4, GTM, Meta, CDP)
attributes.description:
Background / Context:

Current sample request tracking is not being captured consistently across GA4, GTM, Meta, and CDP, leading to gaps in reporting and marketing attribution.

There is also inconsistency between dataLayer event names and GTM tag naming, causing confusion and unreliable tracking.

A key requirement identified is to separate sample-related events to better reflect different customer behaviours.

Scope of Work:

1. Audit Current Tracking Setup

  • Review existing dataLayer events (e.g. Purchase with Sample)

  • Review GTM tag configuration (e.g. sample checkout complete)

  • Audit GA4 event capture and mapping

  • Review Meta and CDP tracking for sample-related events

2. Define Event Strategy & Naming Conventions

  • Align on final naming conventions across:

    • dataLayer

    • GTM

    • GA4

    • Meta / CDP

  • Introduce two distinct event types:

    • Sample Only Order

    • Purchase + Sample (Mixed Order)

3. Implementation in GTM & dataLayer

  • Update or create new dataLayer events for both scenarios

  • Configure corresponding GTM tags and triggers

  • Map events correctly into GA4 schema

4. Cross-Channel Tracking Validation

  • Ensure events are firing correctly across:

    • GA4 (DebugView)

    • GTM Preview

    • Meta Events Manager

    • CDP platform

  • Validate event parameters (value, items, order type, etc.)

5. Global Tracking Alignment (Jaypad Requirement)

  • Review additional requirement to track full order data for Jaypad

  • Ensure compatibility with new event structure

  • Confirm no conflicts with sample-specific tracking

attributes.title: APG Design System Brief
attributes.description:
Context
We previously completed the JAG website redesign, which now serves as a strong foundation in both design and technical architecture. Given APG also manages Sportscraft and Saba, we would like to leverage the existing JAG theme and structure to create efficiencies across brands, rather than designing each site from scratch.


To enable this, we need to extract and simplify the JAG design system by removing brand-specific elements, creating a neutral base that can be re-skinned. Each brand will then apply its own identity with minor variations (approx. 5–10%), while maintaining consistency in core components and functionality.
A work order is already prepared and ready to proceed, pending any budget confirmation if required.

Deliverables

  • Duplicated JAG design system with all brand-specific styling removed

  • Clean, neutral (vanilla) base design system aligned to existing JAG theme and architecture

  • APG-branded design system applied to the base

  • Defined framework to allow 5–10% visual customisation per brand (JAG, Sportscraft, Saba)

  • Reusable components and patterns to support rollout across multiple APG brand

attributes.title: Rip Curl - Deep Dive Analysis
attributes.description:
Files:

  • Google Sheet working file

  • Deep Dive report

  • Oscar’s Tech Audit

  • Rip Curl shared priority & roadmap - Jas has requested we update “Sales Findings” tab

__

Overview
Validate whether performance is actually down ~30% (EU) post Shopify launch vs Magento, and identify where/why.

  • Sales reported 30% down but no validation - GA4 tracking completely unreliable due to:

    • Server-side tracking on Magento vs browser-side on Shopify

    • Consent tracking issues since launch

    • Multiple tracking bugs and implementation problems

  • Launch dates:

    • AU/NZ launched 14th Oct 2025

    • EU/US launched 5th Nov 2025

    • Other markets launched month intervals after

    • Multiple launch phases created data complexity

Scope

  • Sales by country (Magento vs Shopify)

    • Exclude EU countries no longer serviced

    • Check currency accuracy + returns impact

  • Organic performance by country

    • GSC + Merchant Centre (traffic, clicks, visibility)

  • Paid performance by country

    • Google Ads (traffic, CVR, revenue)

  • Sanity check tracking + reporting consistency

Output

  • Confirm if 30% drop is valid

  • Where the drop is occurring

  • Key drivers / issues

Dependencies

  • Required data:

    1. Historical Magento sales data by country

    2. Historical Magento sales data by category (if product categories consistent between platforms)

  • Marketing promo calendar requested for qualitative overlay

attributes.title: Cable – PDP Redesign A/B Test Setup & Mobile UX Audit
attributes.description:
Nicole has redesigned the Cable product page (UX work done by Kate, their internal designer) and is presenting it to the GM and founder for sign off. Once approved, the plan is to run an A/B test between the existing and new PDP. Nicole is meeting with A/B Tasty for a quarterly review shortly and will confirm whether they can handle it under their current plan, but it is expected to be more complex than their usual tests so dev assistance via Dot Collective may be required.

Add this to the roadmap as a placeholder. Target deployment is early April, however timing may shift to post-Easter given Nicole will be away and holiday period traffic would skew results. Confirm with Nicole once she has GM sign off on timing and scope.

As a separate action, reach out to Nicole and ask her to send through the mobile UX audit she completed last year so the team can review before doing any overlapping work on the Cable mobile experience.
